title: Identify Axis Faults and Clear Them in Manual Jog Mode on the Operator Station
summary: Use the operator station MCP_Dual screen in manual jog mode to identify whether Z1, Z2, A1, or A2 shows a fault and clear the displayed fault with RESET.
when_to_use: Use when the operator needs to check the operator station manual jog display for axis fault presence on Z1, Z2, A1, or A2 and perform the documented RESET action to clear a displayed fault.
not_for: Do not use this procedure to interpret the meaning of a fault beyond the displayed Fault indication.
Do not use this procedure as a complete recovery procedure when additional recovery steps are required after fault clearing.
Do not use this procedure for diagnostics not shown on the operator station HMI axis display.

responsibilities: Enter manual jog mode with JOG.
Inspect Z1, Z2, A1, and A2 axis indicators for a Fault indication.
Press RESET if an axis shows a fault.
Confirm whether the fault indication is no longer shown.

safety_notes: This runbook is source-supported as part of a documented recovery sequence and is limited to identifying displayed axis faults and using RESET to clear them.
The source does not provide additional diagnostic interpretation if the fault indication remains after RESET.
Do not infer fault meanings beyond the displayed Fault indication described in the source.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI
Visibility of Z1 Axis, Z2 Axis, A1 Axis, and A2 Axis indicators

access_required: Access to the operator station HMI
system_preconditions: Operator can navigate to the MCP_Dual screen using F3.
The HMI must display the axis area for Z1 Axis, Z2 Axis, A1 Axis, and A2 Axis.
Manual jog mode must be available through the JOG control.

Operator station HMI view showing manual mode axis controls and RESET in the non-recoverable fault context.
Operator station HMI screen snippet showing axis controls, Manual Mode, Fault, and RESET instruction.
Manual jog mode interface example used when clearing operator station motor faults.
Manual jog mode interface reference showing axis fault check and RESET context.

steps.title: Open the MCP_Dual operator station screen
steps.purpose: Access the operator station screen used for the documented axis fault check and reset activity.
steps.instruction: Navigate to the operator station MCP_Dual screen using F3.
steps.expected_result: The MCP_Dual operator station screen is displayed.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator station HMI is visible and the MCP_Dual screen is open.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot access the MCP_Dual screen or the expected operator station display is not shown.

actions.label: Open MCP_Dual screen
actions.exact_action: Navigate to the operator station "MCP_Dual" screen using F3.

Operator station HMI layout associated with the non-recoverable fault recovery context.
Axis display area and RESET-related HMI elements on the operator station screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The MCP_Dual screen cannot be reached using F3.
The operator station HMI is unavailable or does not show the expected control screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: F3 maps to "Visu_MCP_Dual" as the primary screen operators need to use.
Operator station HMI artifacts on page 84 are associated with navigation to the MCP_Dual screen (F3) in the non-recoverable fault recovery procedure.

steps.title: Enter manual jog mode
steps.purpose: Place the operator station into the manual jog mode used for axis fault inspection.
steps.instruction: Press JOG to enter manual jog mode and verify the Manual Mode indication is shown.
steps.expected_result: The HMI enters manual jog mode and shows Manual Mode.
steps.healthy_condition: Manual Mode is displayed on the operator station HMI.
steps.failure_condition: Pressing JOG does not place the HMI into manual jog mode or Manual Mode is not shown.

actions.label: Enter jog mode
actions.exact_action: Press JOG to enter manual jog mode.

actions.label: Verify manual mode
actions.exact_action: Verify the Manual Mode indication is shown.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Manual Mode indication and axis control area.
Manual Mode text and axis display region.
Manual jog mode interface example.
Manual jog mode reference used during fault recovery.

stop_or_escalate_if: JOG does not open manual jog mode.
Manual Mode indication is not shown after pressing JOG.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Press JOG to enter manual jog mode.
Artifacts show Manual Mode in the operator station HMI fault recovery context.

steps.title: Inspect the axis indicators
steps.purpose: Identify the specific axis indicators that must be checked for fault presence.
steps.instruction: Inspect the displayed axis indicators for Z1 Axis, Z2 Axis, A1 Axis, and A2 Axis.
steps.expected_result: The operator can see the four axis indicators on the display.
steps.healthy_condition: Z1 Axis, Z2 Axis, A1 Axis, and A2 Axis are visible on the HMI.
steps.failure_condition: One or more required axis indicators are not visible or cannot be identified on the display.

actions.label: Inspect axis list
actions.exact_action: Inspect the displayed axis indicators for Z1 Axis, Z2 Axis, A1 Axis, and A2 Axis.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Axis labels and control area for Z1, Z2, A1, and A2.
Axis labels and fault-related display elements.
Manual jog mode interface showing the axis indicators.
Axis display area referenced in the fault recovery procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: The required axis indicators are not visible on the HMI.
The operator cannot determine which displayed indicators correspond to Z1, Z2, A1, and A2.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The display shows Z1 Axis, Z2 Axis, A2 Axis, and A1 Axis.

steps.title: Check for a Fault indication on any axis
steps.purpose: Determine whether any of the displayed axes currently shows a fault condition.
steps.instruction: Check whether any axis shows a Fault indication.
steps.expected_result: The operator determines whether a Fault indication is present on any of the four axes.
steps.healthy_condition: No Fault indication is shown on the axis being checked, or the operator clearly identifies which axis shows Fault.
steps.failure_condition: A Fault indication is present on one or more axes, or the display cannot be read clearly enough to determine fault status.

actions.label: Check fault status
actions.exact_action: Check whether any axis shows a Fault indication.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Fault indication in the axis area.
Manual mode axis controls and any visible Fault status.
Manual jog mode reference for checking whether Z1, Z2, A1, or A2 shows a fault.

stop_or_escalate_if: The HMI display is unclear and fault status cannot be determined.
A fault is shown and does not match the limited source-supported handling in this runbook.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If any of the axes show a fault, press RESET to clear the fault.
Artifacts show Fault in the operator station HMI axis area.

steps.title: Press RESET to clear a displayed axis fault
steps.purpose: Perform the documented HMI action to clear a displayed axis fault.
steps.instruction: If an axis shows a fault, press RESET to clear the fault.
steps.expected_result: The RESET action is issued for the displayed axis fault condition.
steps.healthy_condition: The RESET control is available and the fault clear action is accepted by the HMI.
steps.failure_condition: RESET does not clear the displayed fault or the RESET control cannot be used.

actions.label: Clear fault with RESET
actions.exact_action: If an axis shows a fault, press RESET to clear the fault.

RESET instruction and nearby axis/fault display elements.
Manual jog mode interface used when clearing operator station motor faults.
RESET context in the manual jog mode fault recovery reference.

stop_or_escalate_if: RESET does not clear the displayed fault.
The source-supported procedure does not provide further interpretation or corrective action after RESET fails.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If any of the axes show a fault, press RESET to clear the fault.
If the ESTOP is flashing, pull out the ESTOP and press RESET on the HMI screen to clear any faults.

steps.title: Confirm the fault indication is cleared
steps.purpose: Verify whether the documented RESET action removed the displayed fault indication.
steps.instruction: Confirm whether the fault indication is no longer shown on the affected axis.
steps.expected_result: The operator determines whether the affected axis no longer shows Fault.
steps.healthy_condition: The affected axis no longer shows a Fault indication.
steps.failure_condition: The affected axis still shows a Fault indication after RESET.

actions.label: Verify fault cleared
actions.exact_action: Confirm whether the fault indication is no longer shown on the affected axis.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Axis area where Fault should no longer be shown after RESET.
Manual jog mode axis display after the reset attempt.
Affected axis area used for post-RESET confirmation.

stop_or_escalate_if: The fault indication remains after RESET.
The source does not provide additional diagnostic interpretation or next-step correction if the fault remains.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If any of the axes show a fault, press RESET to clear the fault.
Artifacts show the axis area and fault-related HMI elements used for confirmation.

success_criteria: The operator reaches the MCP_Dual screen and enters manual jog mode.
The operator can inspect Z1 Axis, Z2 Axis, A1 Axis, and A2 Axis for a Fault indication.
If a fault is displayed, RESET is pressed.
The affected axis no longer shows the Fault indication after RESET.
healthy_conditions: The MCP_Dual screen is accessible from the operator station.
Manual Mode is shown after pressing JOG.
Z1 Axis, Z2 Axis, A1 Axis, and A2 Axis are visible on the display.
No Fault indication is shown, or a displayed fault clears after RESET.
failure_conditions: The MCP_Dual screen cannot be accessed.
Manual Mode does not appear after pressing JOG.
The axis indicators cannot be viewed clearly enough to determine fault status.
A displayed fault remains after pressing RESET.
The source does not provide further diagnostic interpretation for a persistent fault.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the MCP_Dual screen cannot be accessed or the expected HMI view is unavailable.
Escalate if JOG does not place the station into manual jog mode.
Escalate if the axis fault indication remains after RESET.
Escalate if additional diagnosis is needed, because the source does not provide further interpretation of persistent axis faults.
